guide.title: How to Crop Images Perfectly (Without Losing Quality or Composition)
guide.lead: Cropping an image is one of the simplest edits you can make — but it has a huge impact on how your image looks and communicates.

learnItems.item3: How to crop properly without ruining quality

whatIs.heading: What Is Image Cropping?
whatIs.body: Image cropping is the process of removing unwanted outer areas of an image to improve composition or adjust size. Instead of resizing the entire image, cropping lets you cut out unnecessary parts, focus on the subject, and change the aspect ratio.
whatIs.analogy: Think of it as framing your image after it's already taken.

why.heading: Why Cropping Matters
why.focus: Focus on the Subject — Cropping removes distractions and highlights the most important part of the image.
why.composition: Improve Composition — Use cropping to balance the image, apply the rule of thirds, and create a cleaner layout.
why.platform: Fit Platform Requirements — Different platforms need different sizes; crop to square, vertical, or landscape to avoid distortion.

croppingVsResizing.heading: Cropping vs Resizing
croppingVsResizing.tip: Best practice: Crop first → then resize if needed.

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices for Cropping
bestPractices.item1: Keep the Subject Clear — Ensure the main subject is centered or well-positioned and not cut awkwardly.
bestPractices.item2: Maintain Aspect Ratio — Use fixed ratios (1:1, 16:9, 4:5) when targeting specific platforms.
bestPractices.item3: Don't Crop Too Much — Excessive cropping reduces resolution and may make images blurry.
bestPractices.item4: Leave Breathing Space — Avoid tight crops—leave slight spacing around the subject for a natural look.
bestPractices.item5: Keep the Original — Always save the original image; cropped areas cannot be recovered.

mistakes.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
mistakes.item1: ❌ Cutting off important parts (faces, edges, text)
mistakes.item5: ❌ Using random crop sizes across platforms

stepByStep.heading: Step-by-Step: How to Crop an Image
stepByStep.step2: Select the area you want to keep

useCases.heading: Real Use Cases
useCases.social: 📱 Social Media Posts — Crop to square or vertical to improve engagement.
useCases.website: 🌐 Website Images — Remove unnecessary space and make images consistent across pages.
useCases.profile: 👤 Profile Pictures — Crop tightly around the face and center for better visibility.
useCases.product: 🛍️ Product Images — Remove background clutter and highlight the product clearly.

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.a1: Yes — because pixels are removed, but if done carefully the quality loss is usually not noticeable.
faq.a2: Only if your tool supports non-destructive editing or you kept the original image.
faq.q3: What is the best aspect ratio?
faq.a3: Depends on usage: Instagram → 1:1 or 4:5; YouTube → 16:9; Websites → varies.
faq.a4: They serve different purposes: cropping changes composition, resizing changes dimensions.

guide.conclusion: Image cropping is a simple but powerful way to improve composition, highlight important content, and make images fit any platform. Use the right techniques to turn an average image into a clean, professional-looking one.
